pr.title: Get rid of ctors.s and dtors.s
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed the standalone asm/ctors.s and asm/dtors.s object files by moving their raw section incbins into adjacent assembly files: .ctors was appended to asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s and .dtors was appended to asm/MSL/abort_exit.s. The build lists in obj_files.mk and Makefile were simplified so CTORS_O_FILES and DTORS_O_FILES are no longer separate object groups.
what_changed: Deleted asm/ctors.s, which contained the .ctors section at 0x803B7240-0x803B7260 with lbl_803B7240 and a 0x20-byte incbin from baserom.dol offset 0x3B4240.
Deleted asm/dtors.s, which contained the .dtors section at 0x803B7260-0x803B7280 with lbl_803B7260 and a 0x20-byte incbin from baserom.dol offset 0x3B4260.
Appended the same .ctors section payload to asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s after _ExitProcess.
Appended the same .dtors section payload to asm/MSL/abort_exit.s after lbl_80323090.
Removed CTORS_O_FILES and DTORS_O_FILES definitions from obj_files.mk.
Removed $(CTORS_O_FILES) and $(DTORS_O_FILES) from the Makefile O_FILES aggregation.
smart_moves: Preserved the original raw bytes exactly by moving the existing .incbin directives rather than rewriting or interpreting constructor/destructor table contents.
Kept section names, labels, address comments, baserom offsets, and sizes intact, reducing risk of layout drift while eliminating two tiny standalone assembly files.
Placed .ctors near Dolphin OS EABI initialization code and .dtors near MSL abort/exit code, which is a more semantically related location than root-level asm/ctors.s and asm/dtors.s.
Simplified object file ordering by removing dedicated CTORS_O_FILES and DTORS_O_FILES groups once their sections were carried by existing object files.
decomp_lessons: Small linker sections represented only by incbins can sometimes be folded into semantically adjacent assembly translation units to reduce object-list clutter, as long as section directives preserve the intended output section and ordering.
When deleting standalone asm section files, update both the object file list definitions and the top-level O_FILES aggregation; otherwise the build may still reference removed objects.
For layout-sensitive cleanup, preserve .section names, global labels, ROM offsets, and byte counts exactly unless there is stronger evidence for a typed reconstruction.
Constructor/destructor sections may be better treated as part of runtime initialization/termination areas than as isolated root-level asm files, but this PR provides no discussion beyond the diff.
naming_conventions: Retained autogenerated labels lbl_803B7240 and lbl_803B7260 for unidentified data in .ctors and .dtors.
Object group variables in obj_files.mk used all-caps section-oriented names such as CTORS_O_FILES, DTORS_O_FILES, RODATA_O_FILES, and TEXT_O_FILES.
The PR did not introduce new symbol names or rename the ctor/dtor labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit .section .ctors and .section .dtors directives inside existing assembly files to emit data into the desired sections despite not having separate ctor/dtor source files.
Maintained incbin-based matching for unknown section contents: .incbin "baserom.dol", 0x3B4240, 0x20 and .incbin "baserom.dol", 0x3B4260, 0x20.
Reduced the number of linked objects without changing the apparent section byte ranges or labels.
The diff does not show validation output, map comparison, or reviewer discussion, so matching confidence is inferred from the mechanical byte-preserving move.
searchable_terms: Get rid of ctors.s and dtors.s
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert lbl_803B7240 or lbl_803B7260 from incbin data into typed constructor/destructor arrays.
Search for subsequent build-system changes involving CTORS_O_FILES or DTORS_O_FILES to confirm no downstream object ordering issues.
Look for map or linker-script changes around .ctors and .dtors to understand how section ordering is guaranteed after folding these sections into existing asm files.
